result: There is a tide in the affairs of dust, and I keep meeting it at midnight. While the neighborhood hums with the tired static of dishwashers finishing their cycles, I stand at the kitchen window watching molecules rearrange themselves into the shape of everything I didn’t do. They glitter like cheap confetti, those unopened invitations, unmade apologies, unlearned guitar chords. The moon, a coin someone lost behind the sofa of the sky, lends just enough silver for regret to look picturesque. I brew coffee I won’t drink; the purpose is the aroma—bitter smoke that proves I’m still here, still capable of setting something in motion. Across the alley the insomniac cat performs its nightly parkour, leaping from garbage-lid to garage roof as if gravity were a rumor it refuses to believe. I envy that certainty. Inside my chest a similar jump happens: a small heart clearing a gap it can’t measure, convinced the other side will appear simply because desire drafted the blueprints. Somewhere a 3 a.m. freight train lays down a chord progression of iron and distance; the dishes in the rack tremble like backup singers who forgot the words. I whisper a name that isn’t mine into the dark, testing the acoustics of absence. The echo comes back wearing a different coat, pockets full of seeds. I plant them in the cracked teacup on the sill. By morning maybe vines will have spelled an exit strategy across the pane, and I’ll leave through the green sentence, newly fluent in photosynthesis and forgiveness.

prompt: Write freely about whatever you want for 250 words.
